metadata.title: Skill Taxonomy for Adaptive English Learning (A2-B1+)
metadata.revision_notes: Full expert review applied: cluster splits/merges, GLGR reassignments, prerequisite graph corrections, vocabulary domain splits, coverage gap stubs, duplicate resolution.

sources.grammar: Pearson Global Scale of English (GSE) Grammar Learning Objectives, GSE 30-59
sources.vocabulary: Oxford University Press, The Oxford 3000 by CEFR level (A2-B1 subset)
sources.prerequisites: Derived from English Grammar Profile ordering + Cambridge Grammar in Use sequence

kt_recommendations.difficulty_prior: Use gse_min (not gse_mean) for BKT p_init priors; means are skewed by trailing advanced items
kt_recommendations.primary_domain_policy: Each vocabulary word has one primary domain; cross-domain duplicates resolved in v2.0
kt_recommendations.stub_skills_note: Skills with entry_count=0 are coverage-gap stubs to be populated with authored items before KT training

grammar_skills.name: Verb Complementation: Basic (Like/Love/Hate + -ing, Gerund as Subject/Complement)
Preferences & Desires (Want to / Would rather / Would love)
Ditransitive & Factitive Patterns (Give sb sth / Make sb adj)
Quantifiers: Advanced (A little vs Little / Neither/Either/Each / Hardly any)
Emphatic Do (I DO like it / She DID tell you)
grammar_skills._note: Stub skill — coverage gap identified in review. Populate with authored items.

grammar_skills.name: Verb Complementation: Advanced (Verb + to-inf / -ing with Meaning Change)
Used To / Would for Past Habits
Agreement & Substitution (So do I / Neither can she)
Reduced Relative Clauses with Past Participle (The book written by…)
grammar_skills._note: Stub skill — coverage gap identified in review. Populate with authored items.

grammar_skills.name: Wishes & Regrets (I wish / If only / Should have)
Cleft Sentences & Ellipsis (It is… / What… / Omission)

prerequisites.rationale: Past continuous contrasts with past simple; requires solid past tense foundation
Present perfect vs past simple is a core contrast; past simple must be solid first
Same aspectual form, different tense
Continuous aspect understanding transfers
PPC builds directly on PP; must understand have+pp before have+been+ing
Same structure shifted to past reference frame
Need will before contrasting future forms
Need going-to before contrasting future forms
Past habits require past tense foundation
Real → hypothetical; modality shift
Present hypothetical → past hypothetical
Third conditional uses past perfect form
Past participle form needed for passive
Simple passive → complex passive forms
Advanced passive structures build on basic passive
Tense backshift requires solid past forms
Reported questions add word order changes to tense shift
That-clauses are the structure used in reported speech
Wh-words shared between questions and relative pronouns
Parallel introduction per GSE data (both start GSE 36); weakened from 0.8
Can for ability → can for permission (polysemy)
Must for obligation → must for inference (epistemic shift)
Basic and/but/so → more nuanced result/cause connectors
Result connectors → concession (despite/although) is more complex
Loosely related systems; weakened from 0.6
Have+been+pp builds on present perfect form
If-clause structure similar to when/before/after clauses with future reference
When+past simple clauses require past tense mastery
Adjective suffixes → noun/verb suffix patterns (morphological awareness)
That-clause patterns (GSE 38+) are foundational for 'It's likely that...' modal constructions
Past perfect uses had + past participle; past simple builds participle familiarity
Adverbs derive morphologically from adjectives (adj + -ly)
Cannot compare adjectives before learning their forms and positions
Cannot form superlatives without adjective morphology
'The' is obligatory in most superlative NPs (the best, the tallest)
Direct question forms must be mastered before reporting them
GLGR0098 (asked/told + person + to + VPinf) is an infinitive construction for indirect imperatives
Basic clause-linking (because/so) scaffolds temporal clause connectors
Cleft sentences (It is… who/that) use relative-clause-like syntax
Basic gerund/infinitive patterns before advanced verb complementation with meaning change
Basic gerund/infinitive distinction before complex infinitive patterns
Some/any/much/many before a little vs little, neither/either of
Want to / would rather (basic desires) before wish + past simple / should have (counterfactual)
Basic sentence patterns before advanced fronting and ellipsis
Wish + past simple requires solid past tense forms
Hypothetical modality (if + past) underpins wish constructions
Basic temporal prepositions before logical/instrumental/causal prepositions
Basic spatial prepositions before abstract prepositional uses
Basic verb patterns before phrasal verb particle syntax
